grep_patterns.description: Presence indicates fixed versions (marker set in __io_scm_file_account). Absence when __io_scm_file_account exists indicates vulnerable.
Presence indicates the unix_gc guard exists (fixed). Absence when unix_gc has __skb_queue_purge(&hitlist) indicates vulnerable.
Broad search for the fix marker in struct sk_buff definition. Presence indicates the flag field was added (fix prerequisite).
Presence indicates io_uring file registration exists, which is the prerequisite for the vulnerability.
The dangerous operation in unix_gc. Vulnerable versions have this WITHOUT a preceding scm_io_uring filter loop.
Presence indicates fixed versions (gc_candidates restoration guard).

line_risk_signals.signal: __skb_queue_purge(&hitlist) in unix_gc() without preceding scm_io_uring filter
skb->sk = sk + skb->destructor = unix_destruct_scm in __io_scm_file_account() creates skbs that unix_gc() will mistake for normal SCM references
gc_candidates list not restored to gc_inflight_list after unix_gc() cycle breaking
Fixes: 6b06314c47e1 (io_uring: add file set registration) introduced the prerequisite code path
open() calls kref_get(&dev->refcnt) without dev->lock and without dev->dev validity check
remove() sets dev->dev = NULL then calls kref_put which can trigger kfree via scr24x_delete, without synchronizing with open()
release() explicitly avoids dev->lock (comment confirms awareness of scr24x_delete risk), creating asymmetric lock discipline between open/release and remove
read() and write() check dev->dev but open() does not, creating inconsistency where a raced-open fd can have dev==NULL but still be handed to userspace
Del_only patch removes entire driver files (vulnerable_branch_removed archetype); no guards added
